# codex-swarm v0.7.2

This release adds native Windows setup executables built with the open-source
NSIS installer system, whose license permits commercial use.

## Added

- Separate per-user setup executables for Windows amd64 and arm64 alongside the
  existing portable release archives.
- Windows Installed Apps registration, optional user `PATH` integration, and
  in-place upgrades under `%LocalAppData%\Programs\codex-swarm`.

## Safety

- The uninstaller removes only `cs.exe`, `csd.exe`, its Installed Apps entry,
  and the exact `PATH` entry the installer created.
- Existing user `PATH` entries, swarm state under `%AppData%\codex-swarm`, and
  the optional daemon service remain untouched.
- Windows executables and installers remain intentionally unsigned.

## Supply chain

- Release jobs bootstrap the official portable NSIS 3.12 archive and verify a
  pinned SHA-256 plus the publisher-listed SHA-1 before compilation.

## Verified

- Windows CI builds the installer and exercises install, in-place reinstall,
  version execution, Installed Apps registration, managed `PATH` cleanup,
  preservation of a pre-existing equivalent `PATH` entry, state preservation,
  and uninstall.
- The standard formatting, vet, test, build, vulnerability, action lint, and
  Sonar gates pass for the release commit.
